name: Batu Caves
description: Hindu sacred place

name: Fontana Pretoria
description: "Fountain of shame"

name: Salt Museum
description: Trapani Salt Flats

name: Pantelleria
description: Caper farm

name: Lampedusa
description: Squid fishing!

name: Römisches Amphitheater Taormina
description: Ampitheater

name: Mount Etna
description: Active volcano!

name: Christchurch
description: Anthony gave a talk here at a food and wine convention

name: Fox Glacier
description: Glacier climbing

name: Kisokoma Forest Park
description: Tony did some hiking and participated in a water purification ceremony

name: Ise Shrine
description: Shrine to the forest gods

name: Hanshin Koshien Stadium
description: Hanshin Tigers baseball game

name: Everglades National Park
description: Exploring the swamps

name: Key West
description: He visited Conchtown Cafe, which no longer exists

name: BOTANICA NENA
description: Yoruba religious artifacts

name: Tiananmen Square
description: Sightseeing

name: No.3 Affiliated Hospital of Beijing University of Chinese Medicine
description: Cupping and acupuncture

name: Jingfu Temple
description: Daoist Temple

name: Huanglongshi
description: Old imperial town

name: Culinary Guild
description: Tony taught a lesson at the Culinary institute

name: Designtorget
description: Home furnishings

name: Ponce
description: Tony visited Ponce for the Fiesta of Immaculate Conception (held in December). Street food.

name: Kolkata
description: Tony watched ritual bathing on the river, went to a flower market, and watched wrestling

name: Flower Market Kolaghat
description: Floral arrangements for spiritual ceremonies

name: Picnic Dodol Garut
description: Dodol garut, a rice candy popular in Indonesia. Tony went on a factory tour and watched workers make the candy by hand

name: Lost Lake
description: Tony camped here with Chef Boulud

name: Alter-Action Sandboarding
description: Sandboarding in the Namibian desert

name: Topnaars Trail
description: Tony visited this small village at the edge of the country

name: African Kirikara Swakopmund
description: Sheep and cattle farm. They hunted game and found white truffles

name: Kalahari, Namibia
description: Tony ate his worst meal ever with the Kalahari bushmen: wild boar anus.

name: Yankee Stadium
description: Tony got a tour of the stadium and walked the field

name: Calvary Cemetery - Paul Gauguin grave
description: "He was a cranky pederast and a syphilitic" Bourdain said of Gaugin

name: Pension Temetiu Village
description: They rode horseback to a mountain overlooking this town

name: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
description: Tony went to the Hall of Fame with Tommy, the drummer from his favorite band, The Ramones

name: Zakinthos
description: Tony sailed along the coastline and visited Shipwreck Beach. They grilled octopus and made a seafood stew

name: Green Grotto Caves
description: "The sensible among you will avoid this activity at all costs," Tony says of the spelunking tour

name: UCC Craighton Estates
description: Coffee has been produced on this land for 2 centuries. There is a microclimate that is perfect for growing the coffee

name: Dracula's Nest
description: Estate at the base of the Carpathian mountains

name: Merry Cemetery
description: Every person buried there had a vice.

name: Luang Prabang
description: Tony visited a temple in this small town

name: Mekong River
description: Tony traveled by boat to Ban Xang Hai

name: Ban Xang Hai
description: Tony had traditional Laos whiskey (lao-lao) here in this town that translates to "Whiskey Village"

name: Tierra Bomba
description: Fishing village. They ate lobster at the only restaurant on the island.

name: Plaza Botero - Medellín, Antioquia
description: The city center was reestablished once the drug cartels were driven out.

name: Cabo Polonio
description: "A village of fishermen,  seekers, and refugees" on government land only accessible by sand ferries. They went to a small dive bar where a blind man had a pet penguin named Pancho.

name: Estación Central General Artigas
description: Tony's brother was a train nut as a kid. They visited this old train station in Montevideo

name: Bombay Beach Ruins
description: Tony visited the (mostly) abandoned resorts of Salton Sea

name: Xochimilco
description: Tony hired a boat to learn the history of the canals here, remnants of the ancient Aztec city that originated here (and later became Mexico City)

name: Huamantla
description: They happened to be there on the day of a bull fighting event, where the streets are filled with festival goers and food stands. They ate some tacos. Tony said he was not a fan of the cruelty of the bullfighting but recognized it is tradition

name: Puebla
description: Tony had a homecooked meal with Carlos' huge family. Homemade everything, with Tony's favorites being the tamales and the mole poblano.

name: Samut Songkhram
description: Tony visited during the Songkhram Festival, a renewal or cleansing festival where everyone throws water on each other. It is very hot so this can be enjoyable, although messy.

name: Château Val Joanis
description: Vineyard

name: Aluva Mahadeva Temple
description: The shiva festival is held here

name: Baalbek
description: Some of the best preserved Roman ruins in the world. The town was once called Heliopolis, City of the Sun. They worshipped the Goddess of Love,Venus.

name: Salvation Mountain
description: A mountain painted by an artist using thousands of gallons of paint

name: Anthony Vineyards Inc
description: Tony was invited to the crew meal for the vineyard workers cooking real authentic Mexican food

name: Chornobyl
description: Tony and Zamir visited this site where the nuclear disaster happened

name: Pryp'yat'
description: Tony and Zamir visited this company town which is still radioactive. They were instructed not to touch anything.

name: Kyiv
description: Tony and Zamir attended the Victory Day parade in Kiev

name: Bakhchisaray
description: Tony and Zamir visit Zamir's hometown. They went to a restaurant Tony called Salashihk which I could not find on the map

name: Palacio Nacional
description: Tony met with a journalist here where Congress meets.

name: Laguna de Tiscapa
description: "The highest lookout point in Managua, it was once home to former dictator Somoza's palace and personal prison grounds. A notorious site for political prisoners."

name: Biratori Choritsu Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum
description: The Ainu people were indigenous to Japan, much like the Native Americans in the US.

name: Takinoya Bekkan Tamanoyu Ryokan
description: A high end resort in quiet countryside, "where one lives, and eats, and meditates on a tree or the movement of water over rocks for a couple of days… you lose all sense of time." The innkeeper also served robatayaki

name: Lalaua
description: I am not sure if this is the village that Tony visited, but he said he was with the Makua people, which are in this area. He called the village Buahle, but I could not find it on a map. Tony ate stewed goat, banana curry, and cassava, among other traditional foods

name: Island of Mozambique
description: Tony took a boat out to this island, where he ate lots of seafood like crab cooked in coconut milk, fried calamari, giant crayfish, and other fish.

name: The Garden Of Memory
description: A memorial garden to the slaves who were traded and sold on the island

name: Beira
description: Tony visited during the Day of the Armed Forces, the day the country gained its independence from Portugal colonizers

name: Grande Hotel
description: Whole families live in this old hotel, known as deslocados (the displaced). There are about 3,000 people living here

name: Leila's Hair Museum
description: Zamir went by himself (with a cameraman) to this weird museum

name: Vézelay Abbey
description: Before the meal, the chef brought them to this basilica where he talked about the perfection of the architecture.

name: San Carlos de Bariloche
description: Swiss settlement. Tony says, "I'm frightened of the Swiss" and felt uncomfortable

name: Moreno Glacier
description: Tony went to El Calafate and took a tour to the glacier.

name: Náttúrufræðistofnun Íslands
description: Spa

name: Blue Lagoon
description: Hot springs

name: Satin Dolls
description: Strip Club

name: Aldo Coppola Жуковка
description: Spa treatments

name: Garut Regency
description: Watched ram fights. Visited the snack bar and had some nasi goreng (Indonesian fried rice)

name: Madame Marie
description: Fortune Teller

name: Sewers Museum
description: Public Sewer Museum

name: Anogia, Rethymno
description: Roast lamb in the country

name: Corso Calatafimi, 922
description: Cuci Lampo - tailor

name: Mole National Park
description: The crew are scolded by the guide for spooking the elephants, which is very dangerous.

name: Quincaillerie Dante
description: Kitchen supply store

name: Concordia Stinger Stadium
description: Tony plays hockey

name: Lulea Swedish Lapland
description: Tony hunted reindeer

name: Mondo
description: Rock concert

name: City Palace
description: The maharana cooked Tony a royal meal of goat curry

name: Sant Shree Asharam ji Bapu Ashram, Pal goan, Jodhpur
description: Tony skeptically went to an ashram named Sunil.

name: Jaisalmer
description: Tony took a bus through the desert out to Jaisalmer, a walled city known as The Golden City. Was a major trade route point in the past

name: K-타이거즈
description: Tae Kwon Do school

name: Le Royal Hotel Beirut
description: Tony cooked in the hotel restaurant. The airport was on fire and the there were bombs going off everywhere, but he felt relieved to be able to cook

name: Black Taxi Tour Belfast
description: Tony went on a tour including the Oliver Crowell mural

name: Sundays Well Boating & Tennis Club
description: Rugby

name: Gubbeen Farm House Products Limited
description: Farm with dairy, meat, produce

name: Himalayas
description: Tony had yak milk tea and yak at someone's house

name: Edgewater Pier
description: Not sure if this is the park where they surfed, but close enough. And it was cold.

name: Willard Park
description: They went to see the "Free Stamp" sculpture

name: Zubal Books
description: A very special bookstore with endless shelves of books. Tony visited with Harvey Pekar, comic book writer of American Splendor fame

name: Hong Kong Jockey Club Happy Valley Racecourse
description: Tony lost money betting on horses here

name: New Territories
description: Jackie Chan's stunt team helped Tony learn some martial arts stunts. Then they ate Poon Choi: a large bowl layered with vegetables, prawns, fish, mushrooms, pork, and chicken. A thousand year old dish

name: Estancia Los Corrales
description: Cow ranch

name: Marina di Pisa
description: Tony's friend Nico invited him to eat at his family home in Marina di Pisa. They cooked a bunch of fresh seafood.

name: Restaurant Pizzeria Zum Tor
description: Not sure if this is the same Zum Tor. On the show it was a "gentlemen's club" where men gather, drink and, play games. They played Okey, a Turkish game with numbered tiles

name: CHAMÄLEON Theater GmbH
description: Cabaret dancing

name: Derby Dolls
description: Roller derby

name: Electronic Arts Canada
description: Tony was made into a video game character

name: Vancouver Film Studios
description: Tony filmed a short skit in this film studio

name: Banzai Pipeline
description: Tony rode around on a jet ski here, the best surfing place in the world

name: Lavalleja Department
description: Tony and his brother head into the interior, the country. Met the Anrich family, who live on a ranch called Estancia los Morteros. They eat many things including an armadillo caught by the son

name: Shields Date Garden
description: Date farm with café, gift shop, and gardens. Tony had a date milkshake

name: Rio Chama
description: Tony went whitewater rafting on the Chama River

name: Cavender's Boot City
description: Tony bought cowboy boots here

name: Nippon Budokan
description: Kendo master Tadao Todo did a kendo demonstration for Tony

name: Rokkokuda Temple
description: The birthplace of Ikebana (the art of flower arranging). Tony learned how to make a flower arrangement

name: Sakai City Industrial Promotion Center
description: Not sure if this is the correct address. Morimoto buys his knives from Inotada Knife Productions in Sakai City.

name: El Bullilab
description: Tony visited the El Bulli lab where Adrian tests out ideas for the restaurant

name: Enric Rovira Chocolates
description: Chocolate factory of a famous chocolatier who is inspired by Gaudi

name: International Spy Museum
description: Tony talked with a retired spy and visited the museum

name: D C Central Kitchen Inc
description: Training facility that gives second chances to convicted felons out of prison. They take leftover ingredients from restaurants and hotels and make food for the needy

name: Latin American Gym
description: Wrestling

name: Sant'Erasmo
description: Some of the best fruits and vegetables are grown here due to the great soil. Tony raved about a tomato, "It's nice to be reminded of how good a tomato can be" and "a near religious experience." They also drank Fragolina and sliced salami, good cheese, and crusty bread.

name: Azores
description: Tony's guide Carlos lives in the Azores and his wife makes a Portuguese feast called Terresmos de vinha de alhos, "basically a panorama of delicious pork products, blood sausage, boiled sweet potatoes, and red peppers

name: Furnas
description: Tony was not a fan of the hot spring he said smelled "like a wet fart"

name: Pico Island
description: Tony visited a home on this island. Octopus and octopus stew, pico wine, moray eel, locally raised potatoes, local tuna cooked on an open fire.

name: São Jorge
description: Best cockles on earth, but located in a VERY isolated area that is VERY hard to get to.

name: Tom Tom Tamale
description: Tamale on a hot dog covered in chili

name: Rifka Catering Company
description: They had biryani

name: Sinigama
description: Tony visited this area that had been devastated from a tsunami

name: Foundation of Goodness MCC Centre of Excellence
description: A man named Kushil opened up his home after the flooding from the tsunami and this evolved into an entire network of services including a medical center, a preschool, a business & services center, and recreational facilities free for everyone to use. They also feed Tony red rice, yellow lentil curry, claypot simmered tuna, jackfruit curry, and coconut sambal during the visit

name: Chilaw
description: Tony visited a home here where he was served many authentic Sri Lankan dishes, including the spiciest dish in Sri Lanka, crab curry

name: Buddhist temple
description: Tony paid respects to Mom Gow by visiting her ashes in this Buddhist temple

name: Viña Casa Silva
description: Winery featuring Carmenère wine (local). There was a rodeo on site and dinner of beef, pork, and chicken.

name: Trugo
description: Some kind of weird game with a donut puck and a mallet like croquet

name: Cadieux Cafe
description: Feather bowling: a Belgian feather bowling place with mussels and fries, and live music

name: Milo
description: Tony stayed in this little town with his cameraman for the show. They did woodsman things like chop down trees and eat venison , rabbit, and bacon

name: Yabuli Ski Resort
description: Tony went to this resort just as it opened, so there were very few people around. He did some skiing on the icy mountain.

name: Schoolcraft College
description: Tony visited Chef Brian Polcyn here where he teaches charcuterie and other cooking classes

name: Robertsport
description: Tony had food poisoning when he went to the beach here for surfing

name: Mall of the Emirates
description: Tony went SKIING in this mall. In the middle of the desert.

name: Estadio Latinoamericano
description: Home of the Industriales. It was a dream of Tony's to watch a baseball game in Cuba.

name: Coloane Karting Track
description: Kart racing

name: Macau Tower Convention and Entertainment Center
Porto di Cetara
description: Tony watched as a diver/chef  jumped off the cliff and harvested sea urchin. They ate it fresh out of the ocean.

name: Sky Village Outdoor Marketplace
description: Tony and Josh from Queens of the Stone Age went to this swap meet. They bought some things and ate at the Sky Cafe.

name: Rancho de la Luna
description: Tony visited the studio where Josh from Queens of the Stone Age made some songs for the show. Tony cooked some food: asparagus risotto with black truffle and pecorino. At the end of the episode, they return to the studio where Tony makes spaghetti pomodoro; Dave makes jalapeno cheese cornbread and collard greens; B.O.C. makes veggie wraps with a soy peanut dipping sauce; Josh makes a red cabbage salad with vinegar, cracked red pepper, and crumbled Roquefort dressing; Hutch makes pulled pork.

name: Dara
description: Tony visited this small town in Turkey where they are celebrating a new child. They slaughter a sheep for the meal.

name: Breaux Bridge
description: Tony went to a backyard boil at a chef's house in this area known for its crawfish. There was also roast corn hash with tasso, venison, stuffed wild wood ducks. The next day, he attended a boucherie, which is a pig slaughter where teams of people quickly work on processing different parts of the pig to make products such as andouille sausage, boudin, ribs, cracklings, and more.

name: International Culinary Center
description: Originally called the French Culinary Institute (during this taping), Tony visited Dave Arnold to ask what would Jesus eat for the Holiday Special episode.

name: Tent City
description: Tony visited artists making art in the Tent City, as well as a mass grave that was created after the earthquake. He also met up with actor Sean Penn, who is doing humanitarian work while living in a tent there. They ate chicken pies filled with spicy chicken, picklese, and tomato and deep fried.

name: Bahia bar de kep cocktail bieres belges les pieds dans l rau
description: Tony and Madame Kek took their food they bought at the market to an area along the beach with thatched huts under which they could eat.

name: La Chureca
description: Home for "churaqueros," this is a trash dump that over 300 families live on (picking through the waste for recyclables). Tony was really disturbed by going there.

name: Ybbsitz
description: Tony went hunting in the mountains near here. They did not catch anything but his host's family made dinner with venison for him.

name: Joh. Springer's Erben
description: Tony went shopping for hunting clothes

name: Viennese Giant Ferris Wheel
description: Oldest ferris wheel in existence.

name: West Plains
description: Author Daniel Woodrell lives here, one of Tony's favorite authors. His buddy made some squirrel pot pie, venison steaks.

name: Ilha do Cumb
description: They went here to find acai berries.

name: Ilha Mexiana
description: Tony and crew took a frightening small plane flight to this island

name: WRKO
description: Tony did an interview on a right-wing station here.  The Friends of Eddie Coyle is Tony's favorite crime novel, and this impresses the radio host.

name: South Boston Candlepin
description: Tony played candlepin bowling, where the ball is smaller and the pins are much thinner and harder to knock down.

name: Niseko Ski Lodge - Hirafu ニセコスキーロッジ - ひらふ
description: Tony skiied while his companions snowboarded down this mountain, It had a been a dream of his to someday ski here

name: Green Dirt Farm Creamery
description: Local chefs prepared a stuffed lamb for Tony and Zamir. They served it with potato salad with pork jowl and pickled onions, cider vinegar coleslaw, and cornbread.

name: Kansas City Chiefs Football
description: Tony and Zamir went to a Chiefs tailgate party

name: Rovinj
description: Tony went on a boat to see mollusk alley, where mussels are harvested the old fashioned way. There are also oysters.

name: BIBICh Winery
description: Tony tasted wine at this winery that has the same altitude as the Tuscan region. The owner's wife cooked a 12-course meal for them (although it is not a restaurant)

name: Konoba Sandra
description: This restaurant allowed Tony and his host to cook their fish they caught on the grill. They ate the fish on the boat along with some cheese and bread.

name: Doca de Alcântara / Doca do Espanhol
description: They launched a boat near here to fish for octopus. Tony seemed miserable and cold with his "inappropriate footwear." The crew members then prepared the freshly caught octopus and they ate it with some cold beer.

name: Chinquilho Junqueirense
description: Tony played a game called Chinquilho which involves throwing a 7-lb weight at a small explosive

name: Sauna Arla
description: Everyone told Tony he should do a sauna when he visited Finland. He did it while drinking beer and putting sausage on a grill. He also gets some treatment that causes you to bleed bad toxins out of your back. :/

name: Hyvinkää Speedway
description: Tony went folk racing here in the snow in a VW Bug

name: Popotla
description: Seafood cooked on the beach in this small town. Fishermen bring in fresh catch and cook it for you. They had a huge lobster and a crab, Puerto Nuevo style. They chop it lengthwise, fry it and serve with beans & rice.

name: Monkey Beach
description: Tony went to a tiny remote island called Monkey Beach with lots of macaques. They grilled some fish they bought at a floating seafood market. The island is only accessible by boat.

name: Austin
description: Tony went to a crab boil at a band's house while in town for SXSW

name: Greenhill Farm
description: Lamb and beef raised grazing on the land in the farm.

name: Sydney Olympic Park
description: Tony saw his first Australian rugby game here: The Rabbitohs vs. the Roosters

name: Auxerre
description: Ludo Lefebvre and Tony went there to visit Ludo's mom in his childhood home.

name: Peter Welch's Gym
description: Tony visited a boxing gym.

name: Shark Dive Xtreme
description: Tony scubadived with 350 lb gray nurse sharks.

name: Antica Corte Pallavicina Relais
description: Culatello is made here, made from pork butt.

name: Autodromo Enzo e Dino Ferrari
description: Tony drove a Ferrari on this famously difficult  racing track

name: Az.Agr. Pedroni Di Pedroni Giuseppe
description: Modena Balsamic Vinegar. They had lunch in the attached restaurant: chicken thighs braised in lambrusco; spare ribs slow cooked in white wine with cippolini onions; frittata. All served with droplets of the vinegar

name: Gracie Barra Rio Matriz
description: Tony watches Ottavio's jiu jitsu match, which she wins!

name: Playa Rincon
description: Tony took a helicopter to this beach where they had grilled lobster, rice and beans, fried sweet potatoes. For dessert, pina coladas served in a pineapple.

name: Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi
description: Hotel where he started

name: The Lodge at Mudbrick
description: Tony went boar hunting and then cooked it at the lodge where he was staying.

name: Devi Garh
description: Tony stayed with his guide at this 18th century fort palace (where the guide worked)

name: Kampung Sampireun Resort & Spa
description: Resort where everyone gets a lakeside cottage. A man in a boat delivers a breakfast of rice pancakes with brown sugar and coconut milk in the morning.

name: The Cliff Preserve
description: Tony stayed at this resort in an area nicknamed Patagonia, a mythical land of giants. There is a 5-star restaurant on site that serves ingredients from the property. Steak a la pobre, potatoes, quail eggs, Conger eel from a local fisherman, and a Chilean version of polenta made from corn grown on the premises called chuchoca

name: The Heathman Hotel
description: Tony stayed here, the hotel is rumored to be haunted. Someone jumped off the roof and haunts all the rooms on the way down (all floors with rooms ending in "03")

name: Sorgente Su Gologone
description: Hotel and restaurant that locals eat at

name: Mohonk Mountain House
description: "A weirdly wonderful place" built by Quakers in 1870 by Alfred and Albert Smiley (twins). "Strange, but fabulous," Tony says

name: Hôtel Oloffson
description: Tony stayed here for a week while visiting Haiti and said it seemed like a spooky fairy tale. He had a rum punch at the hotel bar. He also spoke with the owner of the hotel and they shared some fried shrimp fritters and conch in Creole sauce later in the episode

name: Marajó Park Club
description: Tony stays here in this remote eco-resort. He goes on a boat and catches a huge pirarucu fish and the hotel staff cooks it for him over a grill with tomatoes, onions, and cilantro.
